growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based defined medium, maltose only (5 g/L)

up.method: substrate provided at 5 g/L; consumption not quantified as a rate

sec.method: gas chromatography (headspace), H2 yield per cell +/-1 fmol/cell
enzymatic/colorimetric (MAK059 Formate Assay Kit); 0.19 +/- 0.04 mM produced on maltose only

yields.H2_per_maltose: up to 0.5 mol H2/mol maltose (highest, in 0.05% maltose bottles)

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: The cell doubling time was 102 min on formate only (k = 0.41/h) compared to 155 min (k = 0.27/h +/- 0.04/h) ... for maltose only ... the H2 yields per cell were comparable at 9 +/- 1 fmoles/cell ... Formate (0.19 mM +/- 0.04 mM) was produce
growth_records.notes: Batch bottle, maltose-only. Reported k=0.27/h (measured by exponential fit of cell count vs time, Petroff-Hausser count). Doubling 155 min = 155/60 = 2.58 h; check mu=ln2/2.58=0.269/h ~ 0.27. H2 yield 9 fmol/cell reported. Formate SECRETED at 0.19 mM on maltose. H2/maltose yield 0.5 mol/mol reported at 0.05% maltose. Maltose-concentration series (0.05-2.5%) gave unchanged growth (doubling 132-147 min, k=0.28-0.32/h) and H2 yield 15-18 fmol/cell (Suppl Table S3); not split into separate records as concentrations were not mapped 1:1 to each k.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based defined medium, formate only (1 g/L sodium formate)

up.method: formate is sole carbon/energy source (1 g/L = ~14.7 mM sodium formate); oxidized by formate hydrogenlyase to H2+CO2; consumption not quantified as a rate

sec.method: gas chromatography (headspace); H2 yield per cell 98 +/- 34 fmol/cell

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: The cell doubling time was 102 min on formate only (k = 0.41/h +/- 0.07/h) ... The H2 yield per cell on formate only was 98 +/- 34 fmoles/cell and was significantly higher than the yields for maltose only and brewery wastewater only
growth_records.notes: Batch bottle, formate-only. k=0.41/h reported (highest growth rate of the five media). Doubling 102 min = 1.70 h; mu=ln2/1.70=0.408/h ~ 0.41. H2 yield 98 fmol/cell. Formate is consumed (oxidized to H2+CO2 by Fhl); 1 g/L sodium formate = 1000/68.0 = 14.7 mM but consumption rate not measured.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based defined medium, maltose (5 g/L) + sodium formate (1 g/L)

up.method: provided 5 g/L; biomass mainly from maltose/yeast extract
provided 1 g/L; oxidized to H2, not assimilated to biomass

sec.method: gas chromatography; H2 yield 151 +/- 23 fmol/cell, higher than maltose-only or BW-only

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: T. paralvinellae doubled every 139 min (k = 0.30/h +/- 0.05/h) on maltose plus formate ... The H2 yield per cell on maltose plus formate was 151 +/- 23 fmol/cell and was significantly higher than the yields on maltose only and brewery waste
growth_records.notes: Batch bottle, maltose+formate. k=0.30/h. Doubling 139 min = 2.32 h; mu=ln2/2.32=0.299 ~ 0.30. Adding formate to maltose raised per-cell H2 from 9 to 151 fmol/cell without changing growth rate (formate oxidized for extra H2/ATP, not assimilated).

up.method: soluble COD of neat brewery wastewater 60,967 +/- 6,034 mg COD/L; consumption not resolved as a rate in bottles

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: 170 min (k = 0.25/h +/- 0.08/h) for maltose only and brewery wastewater only, respectively ... the H2 yields per cell were comparable at 9 +/- 1 fmoles/cell and 8 +/- 2 fmoles/cell, respectively
growth_records.notes: Batch bottle, 25% brewery wastewater only. k=0.25/h. Doubling 170 min = 2.83 h; mu=ln2/2.83=0.245 ~ 0.25. H2 yield 8 fmol/cell. Neat brewery wastewater sCOD = 60,967 mg COD/L (0.5% maltose = 5,706 mg COD/L for comparison).

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based medium, 25% brewery wastewater + 1 g/L sodium formate

up.method: 1 g/L sodium formate added and inferred fully consumed (COD balance); rate not measured

sec.method: gas chromatography; H2 yield 183 +/- 101 fmol/cell, highest of all five bottle media

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: every 122 min (k = 0.34/h +/- 0.16/h) on brewery wastewater plus formate ... The H2 yield per cell on brewery wastewater plus formate was 183 +/- 101 fmol/cell and was significantly higher than the yields for maltose only, formate only, and
growth_records.notes: Batch bottle, 25% brewery wastewater + formate. k=0.34/h. Doubling 122 min = 2.03 h; mu=ln2/2.03=0.341 ~ 0.34. Highest per-cell H2 yield (183 fmol/cell). Formate inferred fully consumed from COD balance.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based medium, 10% (vol/vol) brewery wastewater only (pilot bioreactor, batch)

up.method: 10-fold diluted brewery wastewater; sCOD unchanged over time on BW-only; consumption not resolved as a rate

sec.method: gas chromatography; maximum headspace H2 116 +/- 83 umol at 24 h

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: Cells doubled every 117 min (k = 0.36/h +/- 0.06/h) on brewery wastewater only ... Maximum cell concentration ... was 1.7 x 10^8 cells/ml ... the maximum amount was 116 umoles +/- 83 umoles at 24 h
growth_records.notes: Pilot bioreactor BATCH, 10% brewery wastewater only. k=0.36/h. Doubling 117 min = 1.95 h; mu=ln2/1.95=0.355 ~ 0.36. Max cell 1.7e8 cells/mL at 24 h; max headspace H2 116 umol at 24 h. Volumetric/per-cell H2 rate not given for batch bioreactor.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based medium, 10% brewery wastewater + 1 g/L sodium formate (pilot bioreactor, batch)

up.method: 1 g/L formate; sCOD reduced ~6% (initial 6% higher from formate), inferred all formate consumed by 48 h; rate not measured

sec.method: gas chromatography; maximum headspace H2 741 +/- 366 umol at 7 h, ~6x higher than BW only

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: every 141 min (k = 0.29/h +/- 0.04/h) on brewery wastewater plus formate ... The amount of H2 in the headspace was significantly higher when cells were grown on brewery wastewater plus formate with the maximum amount being 741 umoles +/- 36
growth_records.notes: Pilot bioreactor BATCH, 10% brewery wastewater + formate. k=0.29/h. Doubling 141 min = 2.35 h; mu=ln2/2.35=0.295 ~ 0.29. Max cell 7.0e7 cells/mL at 24 h; max headspace H2 741 umol at 7 h (~6x BW-only). sCOD balance indicates formate fully consumed.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based medium, 10% brewery wastewater only (chemostat, steady state)

up.method: 10% brewery wastewater feed; uptake rate not reported

sec.method: gas chromatography; steady-state H2 production rate 415 +/- 57 umol/h

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: Steady-state cell concentrations in the chemostat for growth on brewery wastewater only were 6.9 x 10^7 cells/ml ... the H2 production rate was 415 umoles/h +/- 57 umoles/h
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, 10% brewery wastewater only. Dilution rate: pump 5 mL/min into 1.6 L working volume => D = (5 mL/min x 60 min/h)/1600 mL = 300/1600 = 0.1875/h; at steady state mu=D=0.1875/h; doubling = ln2/0.1875 = 3.70 h (calculated). Steady-state 6.9e7 cells/mL. Experimental H2 production rate 415 +/- 57 umol/h (per 1.6 L reactor). Dilution rate marked medium confidence because it was derived from '5 mL/min' feed and 1.6 L working volume, not stated numerically as per-h.

growth_records.medium: DSM 282-based medium, 10% brewery wastewater + 1 g/L sodium formate (chemostat, steady state)

up.method: 1 g/L formate in feed; oxidized to H2; uptake rate not reported

sec.method: gas chromatography; steady-state H2 production rate 5,110 +/- 143 umol/h, ~12x higher than BW only

growth_records.cite: Sistu et al. Formate addition enhanced hydrogen production by Thermococcus paralvinellae when grown on brewery wastewater. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1560780. [PMC11959275]
growth_records.snippet: The H2 production rate was 5,110 umoles/h +/- 143 umoles/h for growth on brewery wastewater plus formate, approximately 12-times higher than that of brewery wastewater only where the H2 production rate was 415 umoles/h ... steady-state cell
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, 10% brewery wastewater + formate. Dilution rate D = 5 mL/min feed / 1.6 L = 0.1875/h; mu=D=0.1875/h; doubling ln2/0.1875 = 3.70 h (calculated). Steady-state 1.7e8 cells/mL. Experimental H2 production rate 5,110 +/- 143 umol/h (~12x BW-only). Adding formate sustained ~12-fold higher H2 output at steady state.
